raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California aims to achieve 100% clean energy and carbon neutrality.",
    "Senate Bill 67 requires growing amounts of California's electricity to come from renewable or zero-carbon sources continuously.",
    "A 24/7 Clean Energy Standard would ensure utilities provide more clean electricity during periods of low demand.",
    "The dependency on fossil fuel power plants increases during times typically low in electricity consumption.",
    "Senate Bill 67 aims to reach 100% clean-sourced electricity earlier than the current target of 2045.",
    "Senate Bill 68 facilitates the creation of all-electric buildings to reduce emissions and fossil fuel use.",
    "SB 68 aims to lower barriers for upgrading electrical service panels in existing buildings.",
    "More than 60% of greenhouse gas emissions in California come from electricity generation, buildings, and transportation.",
    "California had reached 36% renewable energy by 2019, surpassing the interim target of 33% by 2020.",
    "Electricity from all-clean, zero-carbon sources reached 63% in California in 2019."
  ]
}
```
